name: get2know-cms
title: get2know.io CMS
description: Payload CMS + Postgres backing get2know.io (first-party; not published to the public catalog).

defaultEnv.label: Payload Secret
defaultEnv.description: Encryption secret for Payload sessions/JWTs. Auto-generated here (equivalent to `openssl rand -hex 32`). Keep it stable across redeploys — rotating it invalidates existing admin sessions.

defaultEnv.label: Postgres Password
defaultEnv.description: Password for the `payload` Postgres role, shared by both services. Generated as hex (not base64) so it stays safe to embed in the DATABASE_URI connection string.

defaultEnv.label: Publish Webhook URL (optional)
defaultEnv.description: Optional: n8n webhook that triggers the Amplify static rebuild when content is published. Leave blank to disable — it's optional, so an empty value will not block install.

defaultEnv.label: Amplify App ID
defaultEnv.description: The STATIC (WEB) AWS Amplify app that receives the published site. On publish, deploy-static runs `amplify create-deployment/start-deployment` against this app. Set this at install time (kept out of the public package). Leave blank to disable auto-publish.

defaultEnv.label: Amplify Branch
defaultEnv.description: Branch on the Amplify app to deploy to (manual-deploy target).

defaultEnv.label: AWS Region
defaultEnv.description: Region of the Amplify app and S3 ingest bucket.

defaultEnv.label: Deploy S3 Bucket
defaultEnv.description: S3 bucket the build artifact (out/) is synced to before the Amplify deploy — audit trail / artifact store. Set this at install time (kept out of the public package — the bucket name embeds the AWS account ID).

defaultEnv.label: AWS Access Key ID
defaultEnv.description: Access key for the scoped `get2know-static-deploy` IAM user (Amplify deploy + S3 ingest only). Leave blank to disable auto-publish; the CMS still runs and publishes can be deployed manually.

defaultEnv.label: AWS Secret Access Key
defaultEnv.description: Secret for the `get2know-static-deploy` IAM user access key. Paired with AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.
